segments.text: If you want to be as successful as Steve Jobs, you can't just build a social media following. You got to build a movement. This is not about entertainment. It's about
infrastructure. You're one part an architect and one part an investor. On the architect side, you're making sure
that you're working on the business and you're removed from the ops so you can continue to refine the machine. On the investor side, you're very much a
capital allocator. you're constantly thinking about who do I need to go and bring on the team and what is the return on investment from bringing this person
on. Another thing that's going to come up here, right, is as this team gets bigger, one thing you need to focus on is decision velocity. Often times you're
going to have about 80 to 90% of available information to make a decision and you're going to have to quickly be able to refine your judgment and make
the call. If you stew on things for too long, you will collapse.
